frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Honestly, I get that women need to be careful, but sometimes this 'all men are dangerous' narrative feels a bit much. Most guys are decent, especially here in Bengaluru. It's unfair to paint everyone with the same brush, especially when we're just trying to be respectful. Not everyone is a creep, yaar.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment dismisses women's safety concerns by employing the 'Not All Men' trope. It centers the speaker's feeling of being unfairly generalized as a 'decent guy' and challenges the rationality of women's wariness, aligning with surface manifestations like feeling personally insulted when women discuss patterns of male behavior and policing generalizations.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Bengaluru who moved from Patna three years ago. You are on a phone call with your mother on a Sunday evening. She has been alone at home all week. Your father is away on work. You are telling her about the food you ate this week Ñ mostly Swiggy orders and mess food. At some point in the conversation you say something about her cooking that sounds like love and gratitude but is actually a comparison that diminishes every other woman who will ever cook for you and sets a standard no one asked your mother to set and no one can meet.
